summary: Recalibrate ONLY the SLO freshness/latency targets in data/autorun-workflows-v1.json to match the witness cron demotion the founder already approved and merged in PR #91 (2026-07-19, 9a3e3607). The old targets were pinned to the pre-#91 cadences and now guarantee 6 permanent false SLO misses in scripts/autorun_status_v1.py. This is calibration to approved cadence, not test weakening: every new target = worst-case cron gap + a bounded drift allowance, following the existing noos_machine_audit_witness precedent (weekly cadence, target 10140).

argument: PR #91 was a founder-approved cadence decision; the SLO registry simply was never re-pinned to it. Leaving 90/70/130/540-minute targets on weekly/daily/3x-daily crons produces guaranteed red findings that carry zero information and train operators to ignore the critique (false-green risk inverted: permanent false-red). Re-pinning targets to cadence + bounded drift restores the SLO's ability to detect a genuinely missed run: e.g. a weekly witness that skips one schedule now trips at >10320 min, a daily one at >1560 min. No verifier logic, thresholds outside these rows, or success-rate gates were touched.
verifier_instruction: Independent verifier: re-run PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 python3 scripts/autorun_status_v1.py --json on this branch, confirm the six witness SLO-miss findings are absent and no new findings appear, cross-check each target against the crons in .github/workflows/, and confirm the diff touches only the seven rows listed above.
